quality.rationale: 多步自由度计数推理结合局部惯性系物理图像，但黎曼张量部分仅提示公式未给解导致完整性受限

origin.question: Problem 9.22. At a given instant, the coordinate accelerations of $n$ nearby test particles are measured. What is the smallest $n$ required to measure all components of $\mathbf{F}^{\mu \nu}$ ? of $\mathbf{R}_{\nu \rho \sigma}^{\mu}$ ?
origin.solution: Solution 9.22. In a local Lorentz frame, the acceleration of any particle is related to $\mathbf{F}^{\mu \nu}$ by the Lorentz force law:$$\frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \mathbf {x}^{\alpha}}{\mathrm {d} r^{2}} = \frac {\mathrm {e}}{\mathrm {m}} \mathbf {F}^{\alpha}_{\beta} \mathrm {u}^{\beta}.$$It is convenient to think of the 6 components of $\mathbf{F}_{\beta}^{\alpha}$ as the vectors $\underline{\mathbf{E}}$ and $\underline{\mathbf{B}}$ . In the comoving frame of one particle, it has zero velocity and so feels no magnetic forces. Measuring its three components of acceleration gives $\underline{\mathbf{E}}$ . Measuring the acceleration of a second particle as seen in that frame gives only two components of $\underline{\mathbf{B}}$ , since the components of $\underline{\mathbf{B}}$ along the direction of motion of the second particle produces no acceleration. Thus at least three particles are required to measure all components of $\mathbf{F}_{\beta}^{\alpha}$ .The reader is invited to figure out the answer for the Riemann tensor from the geodesic deviation equation$$\frac {\mathrm {D}^{2} \xi^{\alpha}}{\mathrm {d}\tau^{2}} = - \mathrm {R}_{\beta \gamma \delta}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta} \xi^{\gamma} \mathrm {u}^{\delta}.$$
